metadata.source: Grade 3 English final writing and language batch

subject: English
skill: Narrative Writing With Dialogue and Sequence

lesson.story_mission: Join the Grade 3 Writing Studio team to practice narrative writing with dialogue and sequence.
lesson.objective: I can use Grade 3 strategies for narrative writing with dialogue and sequence.
lesson.concept_explanation: Strong Grade 3 writers plan the parts of narrative writing with dialogue and sequence, choose words that fit, and reread so readers can understand the message.
lesson.common_misconception: A writer may list events out of order or forget details that help readers picture the story.
lesson.real_world_connection: Clear writing helps classmates, families, and teachers understand your ideas in every subject.
lesson.lesson_summary: Plan the parts, write complete sentences, and reread with the checklist.
lesson.mini_lesson: Read the prompt, name the writing job, build the sentence or paragraph, then check your conventions.

worked_examples.text: Watch how a writer uses the model to complete narrative writing with dialogue and sequence before answering.

question_audio.text: Watch how a writer uses the model to complete narrative writing with dialogue and sequence before answering.
question_audio.label: Read Question

hint_ladder: Read the prompt and name the writing job.
Use the visual model to plan the missing part.
Reread for capitals, punctuation, and complete meaning.

guided_practice.prompt: Put the lost kite events in the best sequence.
guided_practice.correct_answer: Maya ran to the park; the kite caught a branch; Dad helped her tug it free
hints: Read the prompt and identify the writing job.
Use the visual model to plan your answer.
Reread your answer before you finish.
guided_practice.explanation: This answer matches the Grade 3 writing target and gives the reader enough information.
guided_practice.feedback: Strong writing move — you used the checklist.

checkpoint.prompt: Write a narrative about the beach shell with events in order, details, dialogue, temporal words, and closure.
checkpoint.correct_answer: First, kai searched the sand. Then, a shiny shell appeared. Kai said, "This shell is for you." Finally, he gave it to his sister. It was a day to remember.
hints: Read the prompt and identify the writing job.
Use the visual model to plan your answer.
Reread your answer before you finish.
checkpoint.explanation: This answer matches the Grade 3 writing target and gives the reader enough information.
checkpoint.feedback: Strong writing move — you used the checklist.

game_theme.title: Narrative Writing With Dialogue and Sequence Skill World
game_theme.mission_intro: Practice narrative writing with dialogue and sequence through a guided mission and focused levels.
game_theme.badge_name: Narrative Writing With Dialogue and Sequence Badge

story.text: Welcome to this Grade 3 Writing / Composition mission about Narrative Writing With Dialogue and Sequence. Listen for the writing goal, notice the visual model, and think about what a reader needs. Press Start Mission when you are ready to plan, answer, and check your work.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This lesson teaches Narrative Writing With Dialogue and Sequence. Look at the model and name each important part before you answer. Use the checklist to check meaning, order, capitalization, punctuation, and complete sentences. Press Next to continue.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: This watch page shows a worked example for Narrative Writing With Dialogue and Sequence. Step one is to read the prompt and decide the writing job. Step two is to use the model to plan the answer. Step three is to reread the final answer and explain why it fits. Press Next to continue.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This demo lets you try Narrative Writing With Dialogue and Sequence with support. Read the sentence or paragraph task first, then use the model to decide what belongs. Check your answer out loud like a careful Grade 3 writer. Press Next when you are ready for practice.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: This practice page helps you try Narrative Writing With Dialogue and Sequence. Press Read Question to hear the exact prompt before you answer. Use the visual model, write or choose a complete response, and check the sentence for meaning. Answer carefully, then continue.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: This challenge page asks you to use Narrative Writing With Dialogue and Sequence with more independence. Press Read Question if you want the prompt read aloud. Think about the purpose, choose details or conventions that fit, and reread before you submit. Answer carefully, then continue.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: This checkpoint shows what you can do with Narrative Writing With Dialogue and Sequence. Press Read Question to hear the task, then use the model and checklist. Check for complete meaning, capitals, punctuation, and any special writing parts. Answer carefully, then continue.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: This badge celebrates your Grade 3 work on Narrative Writing With Dialogue and Sequence. Think about the strategy you used, such as planning, adding details, using dialogue, combining sentences, or checking punctuation. Press Next to see your profile and keep growing.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: This profile page shows your growth for Narrative Writing With Dialogue and Sequence. Look at attempts, correct answers, accuracy, hints, and the recommendation. Continue to Skill Practice for focused review, replay the mission, or go back to the hub.
profile.label: Read This Page
